title: anthyllis vulneraria proles weldeniana
intro: Anthyllis vulneraria proles weldeniana, also known as Weld's mountain marigold, is a subspecies of the anthyllis vulneraria plant, which is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the legume family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the production of the yellow dye alizarin, which has been used to treat various health conditions. The plant is said to possess antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and prevent infections, thereby providing health benefits such as accelerated wound healing and reduced risk of infection. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make tinctures, infusions, and ointments, which can be applied topically to treat skin conditions and promote wound healing.
